Lexington Insurance Company'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Feb-14-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Kathleen Harrison, Esq. of HEROLD & SAGER, Tel: 760.487.1047, for defendant/moving party;
Jacquelene A. Marcott, Esq. of MUSICK, PEELER & GARRETT LLP, Tel: 213.629.7831, for plaintiff/opposing party.

The timeliness of contesting the tentative ruling was discussed. Plaintiff's counsel was allowed to argue. After arguments, Lexington Insurance Company's (Lexington) motion to dismiss for forum non conveniens is taken under submission by the court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR# 13859, Email: justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com, Tel: 714.471.2597. (302/RBU)

AFTER HEARING, the Court signed an order granting Lexington Insurance Company's Motion to Dismiss Action Based on Forum Non Conveniens. (See signed order for details of the court's ruling.) (302/RBU)

Lexington Insurance Company'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Jan-19-2023 Continued To Feb-14-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. LAWRENCE A. TABB, Esq. and JACQUELENE A. MARCOTT, Esq. of Musick, Peeler & Garrett LLP (Tel: 213-629-7600) for plaintiffs;
Atty. KATHLEEN A. HARRISON, Esq. of Herold & Sager (Tel: 760-487-1047) for defendant Lezington Insurance Company.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Dismiss Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens.

Argument presented by counsel for parties.

The Court authorizes supplemental briefing by each side not to exceed five pages in length per side which shall be filed no later than February 2, 2023. The supplemental briefing shall be limited to addressing the issues of 1.) whether certain individuals are defendants, and 2.) what the New York court order means.

The Court continues this matter for further hearing to February 14, 2023 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. Parties waive further notice.
